title: hyptis normalis
intro: Hyptis normalis, also known as Mexican tea or false nettle, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of essential oils such as camphor, linalool, and borneol. The health benefits of Hyptis normalis include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Hyptis normalis can be consumed as a tea, taken as capsules or tinctures, or applied topically as a poultice or infused oil to reap its medicinal benefits.
